programmes.show_description: ...Penelope Keith: As the summer ends, rain and storms threaten Mapperton's annual plant fair. Chavenage's sauna opens. And the lakes at Whitmore become a tourist hotspot. (Ep10)
Neil is flown in by helicopter after a head-on road collision, and Matias needs help after trapping his hands in a train door and being dragged along the platform.
John, who's 53, has crashed his motorbike but doesn't want to worry his partner, who's on holiday. Ronald, who's 84, has been bitten by his dog. Gary has a bleed on the brain.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. On day four in Benidorm, Exaucé thinks he has the perfect formula for fun, with cable water skiing. The rest aren't so sure... (Ep4/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Singer Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, with a day of white-water rafting and magic tricks. But some guests are kept waiting at breakfast.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn unwanted possessions into cash. Simon takes Henry to a stately home in Somerset, where he thinks he's unearthed a bona fide Tudor relic.
Sandra and Dave want a holiday home with a sea view and plenty of outside space for entertaining. Danni Menzies shows them what their £180k can buy near La Duquesa on the Costa del Sol.
Bournemouth. Bar manager and musician Adie plans a night of fun, laughter and music, with trumpets and drums. But his tricky crustaceans prove hard for some of his guests to crack. (Ep1/5)
Bournemouth. Aristocratic vet Sir Henry hopes to charm his way to the prize with fine wine and good conversation, which is probably just as well, as his food is a bit forgettable... (Ep2/5)
Bournemouth. Country tweed-loving Pippa serves up a seafood-inspired menu. Hopefully her 'strawberry fish' dessert tastes better than it sounds. Will her night sink or swim? (Ep3/5)
Bournemouth. Spiritual Simone spices up the competition with a Bajan menu. But there's tension when Pippa probes Simone about the colour scheme in her student digs. (Ep4/5)
Bournemouth. Flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes to steal the show with her Michelin star-style partridge and black pudding Scotch eggs. (Ep5/5)
At The Grange Boutique Hotel in Cumbria, Jackie and Nigel hope to impress with high-end suites. But Simon finds his room stifling and the breakfast eggs aren't all they're cracked up to be.
The second visit is to Ruth and Lucy's Rutland Luxury Lodges in Greetham. Most of their guests find luxury in abundance, but Jackie and Nigel get hot and bothered about the bedding.
The third visit is to The Leicester Arms in Kent, hosted by husbands Laurence and Danny. Some guests get a spotless welcome, but Jackie and Nigel make dirty discoveries.
The final visit is to Oakover Guest House in Weston-super-Mare, hosted by Simon Robinson and Ann Wilson. The B&Bers are delighted by the décor, but Lucy and Ruth get a frosty reception...
It's feedback time as the B&Bers meet to complain about breakfast eggs, dirty rooms and sleepless nights. One owner's strategy comes under scrutiny, before the winner is announced.
Metro 6R4: In County Down, former rally driver David Gillanders joins Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw to rescue the 1987 British national rally-winning MG Metro 6R4.
Jaguar XJ-S V12: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Wolverhampton to restore a 1980 Jaguar XJ-S V12, a great British grand tourer that has fallen into neglect.
Lexi hopes to bond with Sam and Jackson. Bobby and Milly try to mend Johnno's broken heart. And puppy Cookie may be too much for dancers Ash and Mike.
While recovering from leukaemia, Toby planned a radical new home - wrapped around an ancient oak tree - to improve his health and provide for his family. But building it isn't easy.
Can Zahid and Ferzana transform an old coastguard station on an exposed East Yorkshire headland into a bold, three-storey glass box of a family home?
This episode focuses on a young girl who fell dangerously at a diving pool, and the difficult work of the hospital's security team.
Tyrell, who's 17, has damaged his big toe playing football. Nicholas, who's 26, has been punched in the face. His jaw is fractured and dislocated and he can't close his mouth.
Paramedics treat a woman who is in agony with a slipped disc, a man with a chronic lung disease who can hardly breathe, and a patient who is unresponsive.
This episode focuses on a young girl who fell dangerously at a diving pool, and the difficult work of the hospital's security team.
Tyrell, who's 17, has damaged his big toe playing football. Nicholas, who's 26, has been punched in the face. His jaw is fractured and dislocated and he can't close his mouth.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. Ray hosts the final day in Benidorm, with a classic car tour. Will it get the group revved up enough to win him the week? (Ep5/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Newly-weds Pip and Nicky welcome their rivals to Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, where a trip to a glass-blowing factory doesn't impress everyone and tensions simmer at dinner.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole turns tank parts into hanging baskets, while Simon O'Brien transforms old insulators into trendy lighting.
Mia has sold up in London and plans to retire to the Spanish countryside near Alicante. She would love a secluded property in the hills for her £170,000. Can Laura Hamilton find the one?
Swansea. Performing arts director Anthony pulls up the curtain on his theatre-themed menu. But will a revelation from straight-talking Tula steal the show? (Ep1/5)
Swansea. Salesman Ross thinks his guests lack class, so he plans a night of culinary sophistication. But he cooks everything to his own taste, including a very rare beef Wellington. (Ep2/5)
Swansea. Social carer Kola hopes to bring some high-class hip-hop swagger to the competition, with a red-carpet welcome, his mum's Nigerian recipes and a very peculiar dessert. (Ep3/5)
Swansea. Triathlete Tula brings a taste of the Costa Brava to Wales, with a Spanish menu and dress code. Will there be a punch-up over the paella? (Ep4/5)
Swansea. Social worker Julie hosts a chilled-out slumber party for the final night. But she struggles to prepare - and pronounce - some of her ingredients. (Ep5/5)
The battle of the B&Bs kicks off at the Masons Arms in Gloucestershire. Can experienced hosts Greg and Vicki make a splash with cold-water swimming and a banging breakfast?
Visit two is to Wards Hotel in Folkestone, where hosts Dill and Darsy have plenty of industry experience. But that doesn't prevent a big mix-up at breakfast time.
Horse racing fans Ben and Freddie hope to offer a thoroughbred stay at their Queens Arms B&B in Berkshire. But their scores could tumble after some cleanliness issues.
The B&Bers embrace the great outdoors at Brendon and Vicky's Midshires Way Campsite in Nottinghamshire. The unique lodges are a big hit; the DIY breakfast not so much.
It's payment day, and the hoteliers have scores of scores to settle before the scores are revealed and they find out which B&B has won a week featuring lost sleep and paltry payments.
Fiat X1/9: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue a Fiat X1/9. Its owner, Steve, who has terminal cancer, desperately wants to see his son drive it.
VW Wizard: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue their first ever custom car - a radically modified VW Beetle called a VW Wizard.
Will Winnie's behaviour get in the way of finding a new home? And Simba looks for love with Jenny, but his instant bond with her brother could be a problem.
Can Colin and Adele overcome contractor issues and financial ruin to build their eye-popping curved glass home in south Manchester?
Brand new series: Janet and Brian's inherited Chinese pottery might be rare... but what are those bubbles in the glaze? Reuben and Lydia race auctioneer Dan for a promising hoard. (Ep6/6)
Brand new series: A patient experiences an unstoppable nosebleed. Paramedics diagnose a heart attack. And a drunk man who's collapsed in the street refuses medical help.
Paramedics treat a woman who's in agony with chest pains, a man with multiple sclerosis who has low blood pressure, and a baby who's projectile vomiting.
